101.description: Yjs collaboration websocket for tmp capability documents

requestBody.description: Create a Markdown-only tmp scratch document. content_type defaults to text/markdown; any supplied value must be a Markdown media type. Canonical UTF-8 Markdown is limited to 1 MiB.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: Optional strict ETag precondition; must match the current document head

parameters.name: X-Quarry-Merge-Base
parameters.description: Optional known version used as the three-way merge base

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Use * to create a new tmp document at this capability path

requestBody.description: Tmp documents are Markdown-only scratch documents. Whole-document writes require Content-Type: text/markdown (or another accepted Markdown media type) and canonical UTF-8 Markdown no larger than 1 MiB.

415.description: Tmp writes require a Markdown Content-Type

201.description: Fork created with cloned version and review history

oneOf.description:
Row-anchored position; present only when the document has canonical
block rows (the Phase 2 review projection).

AgentReviewConflict.description:
A `kind = conflict` review item: a diff3 merge kept the canonical side and
recorded the losing incoming hunk here. `conflict.keep_canonical` resolves
without a content change; `conflict.accept_incoming` verifies and replaces
the retained hunk atomically.

afterBlockId.description:
The surviving block the conflict region attaches after; `null` means
the document start.

baseMarkdown.description: The base (shadow) context the merge diffed against.

canonicalMarkdown.description:
The canonical side that was retained (empty = canonical had deleted
the region).

incomingMarkdown.description: The losing incoming hunk (empty = the write deleted this region).

conflicts.description:
diff3 conflict review items (Phase 4): unresolved whole-file merge
conflicts, present only for documents with canonical block rows.

oneOf.description:
Row-anchored position; present only when the document has canonical
block rows (the Phase 2 review projection).

ApiErrorCode.description: Stable machine-readable codes returned by every `/v1` HTTP error.

ApiErrorDetails.description:
Machine-actionable context for a failed request. Transaction failures
identify the exact op and target; validation failures may additionally
identify the field, rejected value, current value, or allowed values.

allowed_values.description: Accepted values for a closed vocabulary.

current_value.description: Current server value, when useful for rebuilding a request.

field.description: Request field that failed validation or a precondition.

op_index.description: Zero-based index into a transaction's `ops` array.

oneOf.description: Addressable entity involved in the failure.

id.description: Stable identifier copied from the request or current review state.

text.description: Flat block text; all offsets into it are UTF-16 code units.

BlockReviewAnchor.description:
Row-anchored review position, attached to review items projected from
block rows. Offsets are UTF-16 code units; `end_offset` is exclusive.

document_clock.description: The new document clock: the head version id after the commit.

status.description:
`committed` (clock matched the head or was omitted) or
`committed_rebased` (stale-but-valid clock; ops validated against the
current rows).

ops.description: Semantic operations; see the module docs for the vocabulary.

document_clock.description: The current document clock (head version id) the rows correspond to.

PutDocumentOutcome.description:
The document `PUT` reply: the committed outcome plus the merge verdict.
A `PUT` that hits diff3 conflicts still returns 200 — the transaction
that committed is the one recording the conflict — so the outcome alone
cannot tell "applied" from "parked in review". `conflicts` closes that
gap: it counts the open conflict review items this write created or reused;
`conflict_items` returns their stable ids and hunk payloads. Any non-zero
value means the document kept the current text in those regions and the
incoming Markdown rides in `GET …/review`. `changed` is `false`
when a byte-identical write short-circuited without a commit. Raw
(non-Markdown) writes never merge: always `changed: true, conflicts: 0`.
